content.full_text:
Juanita Lira 
From: 
Sent: 
To: 
Cc: 
Advice 
Thursday, November 09, 2017 1:25 PM 
Brian Lau 
Juanita Lira 
Subject: 
AMR!! 
Attachments: 
EVAP Staff Report 11.14.17.pdf 
FYI - see attached. 
Thank you. 
From: LaneDilg[mailto:Lane.Dilg@SMGOV.NET] 
Sent: Thursday, November 09, 2017 1:24 PM 
To: Advice <Advice@fppc.ca.gov> 
Subject: Request for Advice 
DearFPPC: 
I am writing to request advice regarding a City Councilmember's participation in the Council's consideration of 
an Electric Vehicle Action Plan (EV AP) (attached) on Tuesday night, November 14. 
This Councilmember is the VP, Product Strategy and Market Development, for EV go, an electric vehicle 
charging company. The Councilmember also has a small amount of employee ownership in EV go through 
stock options. EV go (hltps: //www.evgo.com/) describes its work as follows: 
We provide charging solutions and stations directly to electric car owners as well as 
businesses looking to serve the EV charging needs of their residents, tenants, emp loyees, or 
customers. Combining your desired tier of service with home, multi-family, and workplace 
charging solutions in conjunction with the largest number of Fast Charging stations across 
the nation, EV go provides EV drivers a truly unique level of freedom-and a whole new 
world of possible destinations. 
The EVgo® network is the largest public Fast Charging network in the nation. From April 
2014 to October 2015 our chargers provided enough kWh of electricity to power 12,323,090 
EV miles. EVgo's forward-looking infrastructure is invested across the country right now in 
more than 55 markets. EVgo drivers can access more than 1,000 charging locations, along 
with individual charging stations at homes, schools, offices, multi-family communities and 
hospitals across our growing network. 
EV go has no contracts with and does no business with the City of Santa Monica. 
On Tuesday night, Council will consider the Electric Vehicle Action Plan for the City. The EV AP provides a 
comprehensive strategy to grow the EV infrastructure system in Santa Monica to accommodate growing 
demand for EV charging and to achieve local, regional and state greenhouse gas reduction goals by expanding 
public EV infrastructure and supporting private EV charging. The EV AP includes short-term and long-term 
goals to increase the number of EV charging ports citywide. The cost to complete all infrastructure 
improvements outlined in the plan is approximately $2.4 million over three years, some of which has already 
been committed. The EV AP also includes a review of existing policies, programs and resources to support 
1 


charging infrastructure on a state and regional level and recommended policy priorities to address current 
barriers to EV charging and EV use. 
Staff has recommended that Council: 
1. Review the Electric Vehicle Action Plan. 
2. Approve the Resolution adopting the Electric Vehicle Action Plan to aim for 15% of sing]e-occupancy 
vehicles to be electric by 2025. 
3. Approve, in concept, charging a cost recovery fee for Electric Vehicle charging so that staff can 
proceed with analysis, internal coordination and community engagement. 
I would appreciate any advice you are able to provide. 
Many thanks, 
( •· c,f 
Snntn ,1,m lt-n-
Lane Dilg 
City Attorney 
City of Santa Monica 
1685 Main Street, Third Floor 
Santa Monica, CA 90401 
310-458-8336 
lane.di lg@smgov.net 
2

sections.question: I am writing to request advice regarding a City Councilmember's participation in the Council's consideration of an Electric Vehicle Action Plan (EVAP) on Tuesday night, November 14. This Councilmember is the VP, Product Strategy and Market Development, for EVgo, an electric vehicle charging company. The Councilmember also has a small amount of employee ownership in EVgo through stock options. EVgo has no contracts with and does no business with the City of Santa Monica. On Tuesday night, Council will consider the Electric Vehicle Action Plan for the City. The EVAP provides a comprehensive strategy to grow the EV infrastructure system in Santa Monica to accommodate growing demand for EV charging and to achieve local, regional and state greenhouse gas reduction goals by expanding public EV infrastructure and supporting private EV charging.
sections.question_synthetic: May a City Councilmember who is a VP at EVgo and holds stock options in the company participate in the City Council's consideration and vote on an Electric Vehicle Action Plan for the city, given that EVgo currently has no contracts with and does no business with the city?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request to FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. The document contains the original request from Lane Dilg (City Attorney) forwarded by FPPC staff with minimal notation. No formal question/conclusion sections are present in traditional FPPC advice letter format. The requestor's question is embedded in the narrative explanation of the situation.

embedding.summary: An incoming request from the City Attorney of Santa Monica seeking FPPC advice regarding whether a City Councilmember with significant employment and financial interests in EVgo may participate in the Council's consideration of an Electric Vehicle Action Plan.
